title: Cymatic Planner FAIL: is_shortest=true but minimality_witness is internally inconsistent (MINIMALITY_WITNESS_INCOMPLETE)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates MINIMALITY_WITNESS_INCOMPLETE: the planner finds the valid flat→stripes→hexagons path (path_length_k=2), claims is_shortest=true, but provides a minimality_witness that is wrong in all three invariants: (1) proved_no_path_shorter_than=1 but path_length_k=2 — underclaims the proved bound; (2) excluded_shorter_lengths=[0] only — missing depth 1; (3) frontier_sizes=[1] — missing the depth-1 frontier entry. The validator rejects the minimality claim. The plan itself is valid and would succeed without the witness, but the cert cannot claim first-class minimality certification. QA analogue: claiming orbit distance singularity→cosmos is 1 (omitting the satellite threshold) — an algebraically false lower bound.

qa_mapping.planning_qa_analogue: The witness claims no path shorter than 1 exists, but the plan has length 2. This is an underspecified lower bound: the witness only exhausts depth 0 (flat alone, no hexagons reachable) but fails to record the depth-1 frontier (stripes), which is the key evidence that hexagons cannot be reached in 1 step. Without the depth-1 exhaustion record, the minimality claim is unverifiable. QA analogue: claiming the orbit distance from singularity to cosmos is bounded below by 1 without proving the satellite orbit is not a shortcut — the satellite threshold exhaustion is the missing link.

validation_checks.details: bfs in {bfs, dfs, astar, dijkstra, iddfs}

validation_checks.description: every plan step is a legal edge in control_graph
observed final pattern class matches target pattern class

validation_checks.details: BFS shortest=2 == claimed 2 (plan is correct; only the minimality witness is wrong)

validation_checks.description: no disordered/out_of_orbit state visited in plan
validation_checks.details: plan visits only flat, stripes, hexagons

validation_checks.description: minimality_witness internally consistent and consistent with path_length_k
validation_checks.details: proved_no_path_shorter_than=1 != path_length_k=2; excluded_shorter_lengths=[0] != range(2)=[0,1]; len(frontier_sizes)=1 != path_length_k=2 — three independent MINIMALITY_WITNESS_INCOMPLETE violations

fail_ledger.message: minimality_witness has three inconsistencies with path_length_k=2: (1) proved_no_path_shorter_than=1 but must equal path_length_k=2; (2) excluded_shorter_lengths=[0] but must cover all depths [0,1] i.e. range(path_length_k); (3) frontier_sizes=[1] has length 1 but must have length path_length_k=2 (one frontier per excluded depth). The depth-1 frontier evidence — proving hexagons is not reachable from flat in 1 step via stripes — is entirely absent. The minimality claim cannot be verified without exhaustion evidence at every excluded depth.
